description: US-SB-008 accepted seeded-serial two-limit hardware validation via hand-edited TTAdapter compiler IR

notes: Barrier-free DES is generated from the compiler-facing NPUIR by removing all four hivm.hir.pipe_barrier ops.
The pipe_barrier_cycles_per_iter constant is fitted from a held-out SEED_N_A=4M source-level ON/OFF run, then validated on SEED_N_A=8M.
Hardware timing uses source SEED_BARRIER=1 with TRITON_KERNEL_OVERRIDE pointing at a hand-edited TTAdapter IR where exactly one gpu.barrier was removed.
Outputs outa and outb are bitwise equal between barrier-on and barrier-off TTAdapter runs.
The standalone edited NPUIR compile emits a device ELF but is not launch-compatible with the current Triton path; it remains documented as a failed lower-level launch route.

blockers: tritonsim-hivm produced barrier-free seeded_off_clean.npuir.mlir and seeded_off_des.json.
bishengir-compile --enable-hivm-compile=true accepted the edited NPUIR and emitted an ELF device object on 910B3.
Replacing Triton's cached npubin with the standalone edited object makes the launcher load it, but the run fails during output copy with NPU error 507057.
A standalone barrier-ON NPUIR object fails the same launch test, so current evidence points to an unsupported standalone NPUIR compile/launch path rather than a semantic failure of the barrier edit.
tritonsim-hivm now emits edited MLIR via --remove-pipe-barrier-index/--edited-npuir-file, but the edited chunk_kda DES has delta_t_bound=0.0 us.
chunk_kda Gap-3 attribution is effectively zero, so the pipe-barrier edit does not move the binding bound by a measurable amount.
No hardware compile/profile/output-equivalence run has been completed for the edited NPUIR.
raise_repeat edits DES JSON, which is an analytical report format rather than input accepted by bishengir-compile.
For chunk_kda, changing repeat shifts attribution but does not change the binding bound.
This changes problem size rather than removing a seeded Gap-1/2/3/4 cause from an equivalent kernel.
